title: About Nexus Telecom
content: Nexus Telecom is a nationwide mobile network operator providing prepaid and postpaid mobile plans, high-speed 4G LTE and 5G data, home internet, international roaming, and business connectivity. Our mission is simple, honest connectivity with no hidden fees. You can manage everything through the 'My Nexus' app or at nexus.example.com. Customer support is available 24/7 by phone, live chat, and at our retail stores nationwide.

title: How to contact Nexus customer support
content: You can reach Nexus support several ways. Call 611 free from any Nexus phone, or dial 1-800-639-8701 from any phone (available 24/7). Live chat is available 24/7 in the 'My Nexus' app and on nexus.example.com. Email support@nexus.example.com (replies within 24 hours). You can also message us on social media @NexusTelecom. For in-person help, visit any Nexus retail store.

title: Store locations and opening hours
content: Nexus retail stores are open Monday to Saturday 9:00 AM to 8:00 PM, and Sunday 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM. Stores handle new connections, SIM replacement, device purchases, bill payments, and technical support. Use the store locator in the 'My Nexus' app or at nexus.example.com/stores to find your nearest branch and check real-time wait times. Some flagship stores offer extended hours and weekend device clinics.

title: Basic Connect prepaid plan
content: Basic Connect is a prepaid plan for $15/month with no contract. It includes 5GB of high-speed 4G/5G data (throttled to 128kbps after the cap), 500 domestic voice minutes, and unlimited domestic SMS. It comes with unlimited WhatsApp and Facebook Messenger and free Wi-Fi calling. Plan ID P-001. Best for light internet users who want flexibility with no commitment.

title: Prepaid Plus plan
content: Prepaid Plus is $25/month with no contract. It includes 15GB of high-speed 5G data (then throttled to 256kbps), unlimited domestic voice minutes, and unlimited domestic SMS. It adds unlimited social apps (WhatsApp, Messenger, Instagram) and free Wi-Fi calling. Plan ID P-003. A good balance of data and value for moderate users.

title: Prepaid Max plan
content: Prepaid Max is $35/month with no contract. It includes 30GB of high-speed 5G data (then throttled to 512kbps), unlimited domestic voice and SMS, unlimited social and music streaming apps (Spotify, Apple Music), 5GB mobile hotspot, and free Wi-Fi calling. Plan ID P-004. Ideal for heavy prepaid users who don't want a contract.

title: Essential postpaid plan
content: The Essential postpaid plan is $30/month on a rolling monthly agreement. It includes 20GB of high-speed 5G data, unlimited domestic talk and text, 10GB mobile hotspot, and unlimited calls to Canada and Mexico. Plan ID P-005. Billed monthly with autopay discount available. A dependable everyday plan for individuals.

title: Unlimited Pro postpaid plan
content: Unlimited Pro is $45/month on a 12-month contract with a $50 early termination fee. It includes unlimited high-speed 5G data (deprioritized after 50GB during congestion), unlimited domestic/Canada/Mexico voice, and unlimited global SMS. It bundles unlimited streaming apps including Netflix, YouTube, Spotify, and TikTok, 15GB mobile hotspot, and 6 months of free StreamFlix. Plan ID P-002. Great for streaming and heavy data users.

title: Unlimited Elite postpaid plan
content: Unlimited Elite is $65/month on a 12-month contract with a $75 early termination fee. It includes truly unlimited premium 5G data (never deprioritized), unlimited talk and text to 30+ countries, 100GB high-speed mobile hotspot, and premium streaming (Netflix 4K, YouTube Premium, Disney+, Spotify). It also includes free international roaming in 20 countries and priority customer support. Plan ID P-006. Our top-tier plan for power users and frequent travelers.

title: Student Essentials plan
content: Student Essentials is $20/month for verified students. It includes 15GB of high-speed 5G data, unlimited domestic talk and text, and unlimited access to education apps (Zoom, Microsoft Teams, Wikipedia) plus free access to campus library networks. Requires a valid .edu email or student ID and renews annually with re-verification. Plan ID P-007. Designed to keep students connected affordably.

title: Senior 55+ plan
content: The Senior 55+ plan is $25/month for customers aged 55 and older. It includes 10GB of high-speed data, unlimited domestic talk and text, free voicemail, large-text billing, and priority access to phone support. Requires age verification. Plan ID P-008. A simple, affordable plan with extra support for older customers.

title: Family plan and multi-line discounts
content: Add multiple lines to one account and save with Nexus multi-line discounts. On Unlimited Pro or Elite: line 1 is full price, lines 2-5 get progressive discounts — 2 lines save $10/line, 3 lines save $15/line, 4+ lines save $20/line. All lines share the account and a single monthly bill. You can mix plan tiers across lines. A family of four on Unlimited Pro pays about $25/line. Manage lines in the 'My Nexus' app.

title: Business and Enterprise plans
content: Nexus Business plans start at $40/line/month for unlimited 5G with pooled data, priority network access, and a dedicated business account manager. Enterprise plans (10+ lines) include mobile device management (MDM), volume discounts, custom billing, and 24/7 priority support. Add-ons include fleet tracking SIMs, IoT data plans, and static IP. Contact the business team at business@nexus.example.com or 1-800-639-8710 for a custom quote.

title: Data-only and hotspot plans for tablets and devices
content: Data-only plans are for tablets, laptops, mobile hotspots, and IoT devices — no voice or SMS. Options: 10GB for $15/month, 30GB for $30/month, and 100GB for $50/month, all on 5G with no contract. Add a data-only line to an existing account for a $10/month device-connection fee. Great for a second device or a home-backup hotspot.

title: Data add-ons and boosters
content: If you run out of high-speed data, add a data booster anytime in the 'My Nexus' app: 2GB for $5, 5GB for $10, or 15GB for $20. Boosters are valid until the end of your current billing cycle and stack on top of your plan allowance. Unlimited plans can add a 'Speed Pass' for $8 to restore full speed for 24 hours during congestion. Dial *111# to check remaining data anytime.

title: Fair Usage Policy (FUP) and data deprioritization
content: Nexus applies a Fair Usage Policy to keep the network fast for everyone. On capped plans, once you use your high-speed allowance, speeds are throttled to the plan's stated rate (e.g., 128kbps on Basic Connect) until the next cycle. On unlimited plans, data may be deprioritized after 50GB (Unlimited Pro) only during network congestion — you keep unlimited data, but speeds may briefly slow at busy times and cell sites. Unlimited Elite is never deprioritized. FUP does not apply to Wi-Fi usage.

title: 5G and 4G LTE network coverage
content: Nexus 5G covers over 90% of the population, with nationwide 4G LTE as a fallback. 5G Ultra (mmWave) is available in dense urban centers for the fastest speeds. Check exact coverage at your address using the coverage map at nexus.example.com/coverage or in the 'My Nexus' app. Indoor coverage can be improved with free Wi-Fi calling. If you experience a persistent dead zone, report it in the app so we can prioritize network upgrades.

title: Activating a new SIM or new line
content: To activate a new Nexus SIM: insert the SIM, power on your phone, and either follow the activation prompt, open the 'My Nexus' app and tap 'Activate SIM', or dial 611. You'll enter the SIM number (printed on the card) and verify your identity. Activation usually completes within 5 minutes and no later than 2 hours. For a new line on an existing account, activate it in the app under Account > Add a Line. Restart your phone if service doesn't appear after activation.

title: Setting up an eSIM
content: Nexus supports eSIM on compatible phones. Buy or convert to eSIM in the 'My Nexus' app under Account > eSIM, or in-store. You'll receive a QR code — go to your phone's Settings > Cellular/Mobile > Add eSIM, scan the code, and follow the prompts. eSIM activation is instant and lets you keep a physical SIM as a second line (dual SIM). To move your eSIM to a new phone, use 'Transfer eSIM' in the app. There is no charge to switch to eSIM.

title: Replacing a lost, stolen, or damaged SIM
content: To replace your SIM, visit any authorized Nexus store or contact support to start a replacement request. You'll fill out a short form, show a valid government-issued ID for verification, and pay a $5 replacement fee (waived for Unlimited Elite). Your plan, number, and balance stay the same. If your SIM was lost or stolen, first suspend your line in the app or by calling 611 to prevent misuse, then request the replacement. eSIM users can simply re-download their eSIM at no charge.

title: Switching to Nexus and porting your number (port-in)
content: You can keep your existing number when switching to Nexus. Get your account number and transfer PIN from your current carrier, then choose 'Bring your number' during signup in the app or in-store. Porting is free and usually completes within 1-4 hours, up to 24 hours in rare cases. Keep your old SIM active until the port finishes. Don't cancel your old service yourself — porting closes the old account automatically once complete.

title: Porting your number away from Nexus (port-out)
content: To move your number to another carrier, request your account number and one-time transfer PIN in the 'My Nexus' app under Account > Transfer Info, or by calling 611. Provide these to your new carrier and they'll handle the port. Your Nexus service cancels automatically when the port completes. Any outstanding balance or device installment becomes due. There is no port-out fee.

title: Billing cycle and due dates
content: Your billing cycle is monthly, starting on the date you activated service. Postpaid bills are issued 3 days after the cycle ends and are due 21 days later. Prepaid plans renew automatically on your cycle date if you have sufficient balance. You can view your current and past statements in the 'My Nexus' app under Billing. To change your due date, contact support — one change is allowed per 12 months.

title: Payment methods and how to pay your bill
content: Pay your bill or recharge prepaid using the 'My Nexus' app, nexus.example.com, credit/debit card, bank transfer, autopay, PayPal, or in cash at any Nexus store or partner retailer. To top up prepaid, dial *123# or buy a recharge voucher and enter the code. Autopay can be set up in the app under Billing > Autopay. Payments post to your account within minutes for card/app and up to 1 business day for bank transfers.

title: Autopay and paperless billing discount
content: Enroll in autopay and paperless billing to save $5/month per line on eligible postpaid plans. Autopay charges your saved card or bank account on the due date automatically, so you never miss a payment. Set it up in the 'My Nexus' app under Billing > Autopay. You'll get a reminder 3 days before each charge, and you can cancel autopay anytime.

title: Late payments, reconnection, and fees
content: If a postpaid bill isn't paid by the due date, a $7 late fee applies and service may be suspended after 5 days past due. To restore suspended service, pay the outstanding balance in the app or by calling 611; reconnection is usually immediate and there is no separate reconnection fee. Accounts unpaid for 60+ days may be sent to collections and the number released. Contact us before the due date if you need a payment arrangement.

title: Understanding charges on your bill
content: Your monthly bill includes your plan charge, any add-ons or boosters, device installments, one-time fees (like SIM replacement), applicable taxes and regulatory fees, and credits/discounts. A higher-than-usual bill is usually from a mid-cycle plan change, an added line, international/roaming usage, or a data booster. Tap any line item in the 'My Nexus' app for a detailed breakdown. If a charge looks wrong, you can dispute it (see the bill dispute topic).

title: Disputing a charge or requesting a refund
content: If you believe you were charged incorrectly, open a dispute in the 'My Nexus' app under Billing > Dispute a Charge, or call 611 within 60 days of the statement. Provide the charge details and reason. We investigate within 5-7 business days and issue an account credit or refund to your original payment method if the dispute is upheld. Refunds to cards typically take 5-10 business days to appear.

title: Upgrading or downgrading your plan
content: Change your plan anytime in the 'My Nexus' app under Account > Change Plan, or by calling 611. Upgrades take effect immediately and the price difference is prorated for the current cycle. Downgrades take effect at the start of your next billing cycle so you keep your current benefits until then. There is no fee to change plans. Contract plans may have terms — check before switching off a contracted plan.

title: Cancelling service and early termination fees
content: You can cancel anytime in the 'My Nexus' app under Account > Cancel Service, or by calling 611. No-contract prepaid and monthly plans can be cancelled with no fee. Contract plans (Unlimited Pro, Elite) carry an early termination fee if cancelled before the term ends: $50 for Pro and $75 for Elite, reduced by $2 for each month completed. Any remaining device installment balance becomes due at cancellation. Your final bill is prorated to your cancellation date.

title: Buying a phone and device financing
content: Nexus offers the latest phones for upfront purchase or via 0% interest installments over 24 or 36 months, added to your monthly bill. Financing requires a credit check and an eligible postpaid plan. If you cancel service, the remaining device balance becomes due. Browse the catalog and check pricing in the 'My Nexus' app under Shop > Devices or visit a store. Trade-in credits can lower your monthly device payment.

title: Phone trade-in program
content: Trade in an eligible working phone for credit toward a new device or a bill credit. Get an instant quote in the 'My Nexus' app under Shop > Trade-In by entering your device model and condition, or bring it to a store for appraisal. Trade-in credit is applied as monthly bill credits over your installment term or as an instant discount for upfront purchases. The device must be paid off, not reported lost/stolen, and have Activation Lock disabled.

title: Device protection and insurance
content: Nexus Protect is optional device insurance from $8/month covering accidental damage, cracked screens, liquid damage, loss, and theft. Screen repairs have a $29 service fee; other claims have deductibles based on device tier. File a claim in the 'My Nexus' app under Account > Nexus Protect within 60 days of the incident; approved claims are often fulfilled next-day. Manufacturer warranty covers defects for 12 months separately from Nexus Protect.

title: Checking your balance, data usage, and useful codes
content: Check everything in the 'My Nexus' app, or use quick codes: dial *123# for account/prepaid balance, *111# for remaining data, *121# for your current plan, and *199# to see your own phone number. You'll also get free text alerts at 80% and 100% of your data allowance. Real-time usage in the app is the most detailed view, broken down by day and by line.

title: International roaming
content: Roam in 150+ countries. Options: a Day Pass at $10/day gives you your domestic talk, text, and 5GB high-speed data abroad; a 10-Day Travel Pass is $50; and Unlimited Elite includes free roaming in 20 countries. Enable roaming before you travel in the 'My Nexus' app under Account > Roaming, and turn on data roaming in your phone settings. Without a pass, pay-per-use rates apply, so a pass is strongly recommended. Wi-Fi calling works abroad at no charge over Wi-Fi.

title: International calling rates from home
content: Call internationally from your home network with the World Calling add-on: $10/month for discounted rates and 300 minutes to 60+ countries, or pay-per-minute without the add-on (rates vary by country, shown in the app before you call). Unlimited Pro includes unlimited calls to Canada and Mexico; Unlimited Elite includes unlimited calls to 30+ countries. Calls over Wi-Fi to Wi-Fi apps are always free. Check specific country rates at nexus.example.com/international.

title: Voicemail setup and access
content: Voicemail is free on all plans. Set it up by pressing and holding 1 on your dialer and following the prompts to record a greeting and set a PIN. Access voicemail anytime by holding 1, or use Visual Voicemail in the 'My Nexus' app to read and manage messages as a list. To reset a forgotten voicemail PIN, go to the app under Account > Voicemail or call 611.

title: Call features: caller ID, forwarding, conference, and ringback tones
content: All plans include caller ID, call waiting, and 3-way conference calling at no charge. Set up call forwarding by dialing *72 followed by the number (turn off with *73). Ringback/Caller Tunes let callers hear a song instead of ringing — subscribe for $2/month in the app under Services > Caller Tunes. Enhanced Caller ID with spam labeling is included free (see spam blocking).

title: Wi-Fi calling setup
content: Wi-Fi calling lets you make and receive calls and texts over any Wi-Fi network — great for weak indoor coverage. It's free and included on all plans. Enable it in your phone settings (Settings > Cellular/Phone > Wi-Fi Calling) and enter/confirm your emergency address. Calls over Wi-Fi to domestic numbers are free; while abroad, Wi-Fi calling to domestic numbers is also free over Wi-Fi.

title: Spam and scam call blocking
content: Nexus Call Guard is included free and automatically labels or blocks suspected spam and scam calls. Manage it in the 'My Nexus' app under Services > Call Guard, where you can enable auto-blocking, report numbers, and maintain a personal block list. For stronger filtering, Call Guard Plus ($3/month) sends unknown callers to a screening service. You can also block any individual number from your recent-calls list.

title: Parental controls and content filtering
content: Nexus Family Safety lets account owners set content filters, app-time limits, and location alerts for lines on the account — ideal for kids' phones. Enable it in the 'My Nexus' app under Account > Family Safety. You can filter adult content, set daily screen-time schedules, pause internet on a line, and see a line's location. Family Safety is free on Unlimited plans and $5/month otherwise.

title: Lost or stolen phone: suspend and protect your line
content: If your phone is lost or stolen, immediately suspend your line to prevent misuse: open the 'My Nexus' app on another device or call 611 and choose Suspend Line. Suspension stops calls, texts, and data while keeping your number reserved. You can request a replacement SIM/eSIM to restore service on a new device. If you have Nexus Protect, file a loss/theft claim for a replacement device. Report theft to local police for your records.

title: Data rollover
content: Unused high-speed data on eligible capped plans (Prepaid Plus, Prepaid Max, Essential) rolls over once to the next billing cycle, up to your plan's monthly allowance. Rollover data is used after your fresh monthly allowance and expires at the end of the next cycle. Unlimited plans don't include rollover since data is already unlimited. Check your rollover balance in the 'My Nexus' app under Billing > Data.

title: Nexus Rewards loyalty program
content: Nexus Rewards is our free loyalty program. Earn points for on-time payments, app check-ins, and tenure, and redeem them for bill credits, device discounts, data boosters, and partner offers (coffee, streaming, travel). Long-tenure members unlock perks like waived fees and priority support. Join and track points in the 'My Nexus' app under Rewards. Points never expire while your account is active.

title: Refer-a-friend program
content: Refer friends to Nexus and you both get a $25 bill credit once they activate a new line and stay active for 30 days. Share your unique referral link from the 'My Nexus' app under Rewards > Refer a Friend. You can earn up to $250 in referral credits per year. Credits appear on your bill within one cycle after your friend qualifies.

title: Discounts: student, military, and first responder
content: Nexus offers 15% off eligible unlimited plans for active/veteran military, first responders (police, fire, EMT), nurses, and teachers, and a dedicated Student Essentials plan for students. Verify your eligibility in the 'My Nexus' app under Account > Discounts through our verification partner. Discounts apply to the plan charge on up to 4 lines and can't be combined with certain promotions.

title: Account security, PIN, and password reset
content: Protect your account with a security PIN and two-factor authentication, set under Account > Security in the 'My Nexus' app. Your PIN is required for sensitive changes like SIM swaps and port-outs, which helps prevent SIM-swap fraud. To reset your app password, tap 'Forgot password' at sign-in and verify by SMS or email. If you suspect unauthorized access, call 611 immediately to lock your account.

title: Troubleshooting: no signal or no service
content: If you see 'No Service' or SOS: 1) Toggle Airplane Mode on for 10 seconds, then off. 2) Restart your phone. 3) Check that your SIM/eSIM is active and seated properly. 4) Ensure network selection is set to Automatic and mobile data/roaming is on. 5) Check for an outage or coverage gap on nexus.example.com/coverage. 6) Update carrier settings when prompted. If still down after these steps, enable Wi-Fi calling and contact 611 to check your line status.

title: Troubleshooting: slow data and APN settings
content: For slow data: 1) Confirm you haven't hit your high-speed cap (dial *111#) — add a booster if needed. 2) Restart your phone and toggle mobile data. 3) Check you're on 5G/LTE, not limited to 3G, in network settings. 4) Reset the APN to defaults — set APN to 'nexus' (Settings > Cellular > APN) or select 'Reset to default'. 5) Try a different location, as congestion or building materials can slow speeds. Wi-Fi calling and Wi-Fi data can help indoors.

title: Troubleshooting: can't send or receive SMS/MMS
content: If texts won't send/receive: 1) Restart your phone. 2) Ensure you have signal and your line is active. 3) For MMS (picture/group messages), mobile data must be ON even on Wi-Fi. 4) Check the recipient number format and that you haven't blocked them. 5) Clear your messaging app cache or try the default messaging app. 6) Confirm the message centre number is correct (reset carrier settings). Still stuck? Call 611 to check for message-blocking on the line.

title: Emergency calls and 911
content: You can always call emergency services (911) even with no plan balance, a locked phone, or 'No Service' if any network is available. For Wi-Fi calling, keep your registered emergency address up to date so responders are routed correctly. Nexus supports Enhanced 911 location and emergency text-to-911 where available. Emergency calls are never charged.

title: Network outages and service status
content: Check current network status and planned maintenance at nexus.example.com/status or in the 'My Nexus' app under Support > Network Status. Enter your address to see if a local outage is affecting you and get restoration estimates. During a confirmed outage, Wi-Fi calling keeps you connected over any Wi-Fi. If an outage significantly affects you, you may be eligible for an account credit — contact 611 after service is restored.

title: Accessibility services
content: Nexus provides accessibility support including large-text and braille billing, TTY/RTT calling, compatibility with screen readers, and specialized support agents. Enable large-text billing in the app under Account > Accessibility. Data plans for connected hearing/medical devices are available. Contact our accessibility team via 611 (say 'accessibility') for personalized help setting up your service.

title: Prepaid vs postpaid: which to choose
content: Prepaid means you pay in advance each cycle with no contract, no credit check, and easy budgeting — ideal for flexibility. Postpaid bills you at the end of each cycle, supports device financing and multi-line family discounts, and often includes more perks — ideal if you want unlimited data, streaming bundles, or to finance a phone. You can switch between prepaid and postpaid while keeping your number; contact 611 or use the app to change.

title: Shared data and data gifting
content: On multi-line accounts, you can share a data pool or gift data between lines. Enable data sharing in the 'My Nexus' app under Billing > Data > Share, or gift a data booster to another line on your account instantly. Account owners control which lines can share and can set per-line data limits so one line doesn't use it all. Gifting is free; the gifted data follows your plan's validity.

title: Home Internet from Nexus
content: Nexus 5G Home Internet delivers wireless broadband with no annual contract from $50/month (or $40/month bundled with a mobile plan). It includes a free Wi-Fi 6 gateway, unlimited data, and typical speeds of 100-300 Mbps depending on coverage. Setup is self-install in minutes — plug in the gateway and follow the app. Check availability at your address at nexus.example.com/home.
